rulings.Case Title: PHILADELPHIA INDEMNITY INSURANCE COMPANY VS. RICHARD GREENLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Numbers 1.1, 2.6, 15.1, 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5 And 50.6, Request For Production Of Documents Set One, Numbers 1 And 2; And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2435
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT JOHANNES HUWYLER Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Numbers 1.1, 2.6, 15.1, 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5 And 50.6, Request For Production Of Documents Set One, Numbers 1 And 2; And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2435. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA DAVIDSON VS. MERCEDES-BENZ USA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Demurrer overruled. The Ninth and Tenth Causes of Action state claims for breach of contract and breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing, respectively, and are not uncertain. The Second Amended Complaint (SAC) alleges that a fire destroyed Plaintiff's car, causing loss to Plaintiff. (See, e.g., SAC at 17.) The cause of the fire is a factual issue and therefore it cannot be determined based on the pleadings whether Plaintiff's policy included coverage of the loss. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ASSET CAPITAL RECOVERY GROUP, LLC VS. MARY E. ROGERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF ASSET CAPITAL RECOVERY GROUP, LLC's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Opposition Hearing required. The Court cannot determine whether Judgment Debtor is seeking relief under CCP section 704.115 or 704.110. Further, Judgment Debtor must provide evidence that the amount levied from her bank account includes payments of private benefits, public benefits, Social Security benefits, and/or qualifying retirement plan distributions pursuant to CCP sections 704.115, 704.110 and/or 704.080. Defendant is requested to bring to the hearing bank account statements showing withdrawals and deposits and evidence of public benefits payments, social security benefits payments, and/or qualifying retirement plan distributions. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: VANESSA YOUNG VS. REMX, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, DBA REMX et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Individual Arbitration, Dismissal Of Pltff'S Class Claims, Bifurcation Of Pltff'S Paga Claim And Stay Of Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 14, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT REMEDY TEMPORARY SERVICES LLC's Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Individual Arbitration, Dismissal Of Plaintiff's Class Claims, Bifurcation Of Plaintiff's Paga Claim And Stay Of Proceedings Granted. Plaintiff and Defendant moving party entered into a binding and enforceable arbitration agreement ("Arbitratation Agreement") that included a class action waiver provision. The Arbitration Agreement covers the failure to timely pay final wages upon termination claim that is at issue in Plaintiff's lawsuit. The Arbitration Agreement is not unconscionable. The AAA rules comport with the minimum requirements set forth in Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. Plaintiff Young's vague declaration is insufficient to establish unconscionability. The class action waiver in the Arbitration Agreement is enforceable. (See Iskanian v. CLS Transportation Los Angeles, LLC (2014) 59 Cal. 4th 348.) Pursuant to the Arbitration Agreement, the first and second causes of action shall be resolved through individual arbitration. The class claims are dismissed pursuant to the arbitration agreement's class action waiver provision. Plaintiff's representative claim pursuant to the Private Atorneys General Act of 2004 ("PAGA") shall be stayed pending the completion of arbitration. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, filed August 15, 2014 is granted. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice in Support of Its Reply Brief is denied. To the extent admissibility is sought pursuant to Evidence Code section 452(d), the documents are not relevant. Further, the documents do not meet the requirements of Evidence Code section 452(h), the alternative basis for admissibility. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the specific portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
